topic: rice price going up — Lan's stall costs

turns.content: you know what make me angry today friend. rice price go up again. 18,500 dong per kilo now. last month 16,000. crazy
That's a big jump — about 15% in a single month. What's driving it up in your area? Is it a supply issue or are the middlemen pushing prices?
they say flood damage up north, harvest low. but I think middleman keep extra profit too, you know. always same story. farmer get hurt both side — buy expensive, sell cheap

Yeah, farmers always get squeezed on both ends — input costs go up but selling prices don't move the same way. The flood damage explanation makes some sense for a short-term spike, but you're right that middlemen tend to add their margin on top of any real shortage.

Is the rice price affecting your costs directly? Feed, household expenses?

everything, friend. rice for eat at home more expensive. my wife she complain because the stall cost go up too, you know. she have to buy rice for cook, for customer. when rice price go up, everything follow

That makes sense — rice is such a foundation item that when it moves, everything else moves with it. Food stall costs especially, since rice is probably one of the biggest ingredients by volume.

Have you thought about buying in bulk to lock in a price before it goes higher? Sometimes buying a few extra bags now saves money if the trend keeps going up.

I think about that. but storage problem, you know. this weather so humid, rice get mold fast if not store right. last time I buy too much, lose one bag because bug get in. 50 kilo bag, just waste. that make me more angry than expensive price

Ugh, losing a whole 50kg bag to bugs is painful. That's basically throwing money away.

For storage in humid conditions, a few cheap things help a lot:

- **Airtight plastic drums** or bins with tight lids — keeps bugs and moisture out. You can get 100-liter drums at agricultural supply shops.
- **Elevate off the floor** — put bags on wooden pallets or a shelf, not directly on concrete or dirt. Moisture wicks up from below.
- **Bay leaves or neem leaves** mixed into the rice can repel weevils naturally.

If you buy 2-3 bags at the current price and store them properly, you'd save money if prices keep climbing for the next month or two.
bay leaves? really? my wife use bay leaf for cooking, you know. never think to put in rice storage. that actually work?

Yes, it really works! Bay leaves contain compounds like eugenol and linalool that weevils and grain beetles hate. Just scatter 5-6 dried bay leaves per bag of rice. They won't affect the taste of the rice at all — you'd remove them before cooking anyway.

Some people also use dried chili peppers the same way. The capsaicin repels bugs too. If your wife already has bay leaves in the kitchen, it costs you nothing to try.

plastic drum I have some. use for fish sauce before, you know. maybe I clean and use for rice. put bay leaf inside. but still the problem is money going out too fast. feed cost, fuel cost, now rice cost. everything up. income same

That's the tightest spot to be in — costs rising on all fronts while revenue stays flat. It's the same squeeze farmers everywhere deal with, and there's no easy answer.

The old fish sauce drums would work fine for rice storage — just wash them out well and dry completely in the sun before putting rice in. The thick plastic keeps humidity out really well.

Are there any costs you've been able to cut back on, or is everything pretty much essential?
cut what, friend? feed I cannot cut, shrimp die. fuel for aerator I cannot cut, shrimp die. rice I cannot cut, family die. hah. I just work more, eat less, you know. that is farmer life

Ha — that's blunt but accurate. You can't cut the things that keep your stock and your family alive.

One thing to keep an eye on: rice prices usually come back down once the next harvest comes in, so this spike might be temporary. If you can ride it out with some bulk buying in those drums, the pressure should ease in a few weeks.

Hang in there. Hard season doesn't last forever.

you right. every season pass eventually. just this one feel heavier, you know. everything at same time. but okay. I survive worse. I go clean those drum now. maybe buy 3 bag tomorrow before price go up more

That's the spirit — you've been through worse and you're still standing. 3 bags in clean drums with some bay leaves should hold you for a while. Dry the drums in the sun first and keep them off the ground.

Good luck with the prices. Hope they come down soon.
thank you friend. small thing but it help to talk. okay bye bye. rice not getting cheaper while I sit here. hah
Ha — true enough. Go get those drums ready. Take care, and I hope next month the prices ease up.
